member.name: T:NetMediate.Guard
member.summary: Provides guard helper methods for argument validation across framework targets.

member.name: M:NetMediate.Guard.ThrowIfNull(System.Object,System.String)
member.summary: Throws when the provided argument is null.
member.param: Argument instance to validate.
Optional argument name.

member.name: T:NetMediate.ICommand
member.summary: Represents a command that can be executed within an application.
member.remarks: Implement this interface to define operations or actions that encapsulate a request as an object. This pattern is commonly used to decouple the sender of a request from its handler, enabling features such as undo/redo, logging, or queuing of operations.

member.name: T:NetMediate.ICommandHandler`1
member.summary: Defines a handler for processing a command message asynchronously.
member.remarks: Implement this interface to provide custom logic for handling command messages. The handler executes the command and returns a representing the asynchronous operation.
member.typeparam: The type of command message to handle. Must not be null.

member.name: T:NetMediate.IHandler`2
member.summary: Defines a handler that processes a message of a specified type and returns a result.
member.remarks: Implementations should ensure thread safety if handlers are used concurrently. The handler is responsible for processing the message and returning an appropriate result. Cancellation is supported via the provided token.
member.typeparam: The type of message to be handled. Must not be null.
The type of result produced by handling the message.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IHandler`2.Handle(`0,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Handles the specified message and returns a result of the operation.
member.param: The message to be processed. Cannot be null.
A cancellation token that can be used to cancel the operation.
member.returns: A result of type produced by handling the message.

member.name: T:NetMediate.IMediator
member.summary: Defines a mediator for sending messages, notifications, and requests between components.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediator.Notify``1(``0,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Publishes a notification to all registered handlers.
member.typeparam: The type of the notification message.
member.param: The notification message to publish.
A token to observe while waiting for the task to complete.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediator.Notify``1(System.Object,``0,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Publishes a notification to all registered handlers.
member.typeparam: The type of the notification message.
member.param: An optional key to distinguish this notification from others of the same message type.
The notification message to publish.
A token to observe while waiting for the task to complete.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediator.Notify``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Publishes a collection of notification messages to all registered handlers.
member.typeparam: The type of the notification message.
member.param: The collection of notification messages to publish.
A token to observe while waiting for the task to complete.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediator.Notify``1(System.Object,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Publishes a collection of notification messages to all registered handlers.
member.typeparam: The type of the notification message.
member.param: An optional key to distinguish this notification from others of the same message type.
The collection of notification messages to publish.
A token to observe while waiting for the task to complete.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediator.Send``1(``0,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends a command to all registered handlers in parallel.
member.remarks: All registered implementations receive the command concurrently via Task.WhenAll. Use this when you want to trigger an action across multiple consumers with no return value.
member.typeparam: The type of the command message.
member.param: The command to send.
A token to observe while waiting for the task to complete.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediator.Send``1(System.Object,``0,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends a command to all registered handlers in parallel.
member.remarks: All registered implementations receive the command concurrently via Task.WhenAll. Use this when you want to trigger an action across multiple consumers with no return value.
member.typeparam: The type of the command message.
member.param: An optional key to distinguish this command from others of the same message type.
The command to send.
A token to observe while waiting for the task to complete.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediator.Send``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends a command to all registered handlers in parallel.
member.remarks: All registered implementations receive the command concurrently via Task.WhenAll. Use this when you want to trigger an action across multiple consumers with no return value.
member.typeparam: The type of the command message.
member.param: The command to send.
A token to observe while waiting for the task to complete.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediator.Send``1(System.Object,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends a command to all registered handlers in parallel.
member.remarks: All registered implementations receive the command concurrently via Task.WhenAll. Use this when you want to trigger an action across multiple consumers with no return value.
member.typeparam: The type of the command message.
member.param: An optional key to distinguish this command from others of the same message type.
The command to send.
A token to observe while waiting for the task to complete.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediator.Request``2(``0,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends a request to a handler and awaits a response.
member.typeparam: The type of the request message.
The type of the response.
member.param: The request to send.
A token to observe while waiting for the task to complete.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation and contains the response.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediator.Request``2(System.Object,``0,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends a request to a handler and awaits a response.
member.typeparam: The type of the request message.
The type of the response.
member.param: An optional key to distinguish this request from others of the same message type.
The request to send.
A token to observe while waiting for the task to complete.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation and contains the response.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediator.RequestStream``2(``0,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends a request to a handler and receives a stream of responses asynchronously.
member.typeparam: The type of the request message.
The type of the response items in the stream.
member.param: The request to send.
A token to observe while waiting for the stream to complete.
member.returns: An asynchronous stream of responses.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediator.RequestStream``2(System.Object,``0,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends a request to a handler and receives a stream of responses asynchronously.
member.typeparam: The type of the request message.
The type of the response items in the stream.
member.param: An optional key to distinguish this request from others of the same message type.
The request to send.
A token to observe while waiting for the stream to complete.
member.returns: An asynchronous stream of responses.

member.name: T:NetMediate.IMediatorServiceBuilder
member.summary: Defines a builder interface for registering handlers in the mediator service. This interface allows for fluent configuration of message handlers, enabling the registration of handlers for specific message types and result types. Handlers can be registered with or without custom instantiation logic, providing flexibility in how handlers are created and managed within the mediator framework.

member.name: P:NetMediate.IMediatorServiceBuilder.Services
member.summary: Gets the collection of service descriptors for dependency injection configuration.
member.remarks: Use this property to register application services, configure dependencies, or modify the service collection before building the service provider. The returned collection is typically used during application startup or module initialization.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediatorServiceBuilder.RegisterHandler``4(System.Object)
member.summary: Registers a handler for a specific message type and result type.
member.typeparam: The interface type that the handler implements.
The concrete handler type.
The message type.
The result type.
member.param: An optional key to distinguish this handler from others of the same interface type.
member.returns: The current instance of for chaining.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediatorServiceBuilder.RegisterCommandHandler``2(System.Object)
member.summary: Registers a command handler and its closed-type pipeline executor. Prefer this over for commands.
member.param: An optional key to distinguish this handler from others of the same interface type.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediatorServiceBuilder.RegisterNotificationHandler``2(System.Object)
member.summary: Registers a notification handler and its closed-type pipeline executor.
member.param: An optional key to distinguish this handler from others of the same interface type.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediatorServiceBuilder.RegisterRequestHandler``3(System.Object)
member.summary: Registers a request handler and its closed-type pipeline executor.
member.param: An optional key to distinguish this handler from others of the same interface type.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediatorServiceBuilder.RegisterStreamHandler``3(System.Object)
member.summary: Registers a stream handler and its closed-type pipeline executor.
member.param: An optional key to distinguish this handler from others of the same interface type.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediatorServiceBuilder.RegisterBehavior``3
member.summary: Registers a pipeline behavior for a specific message type and result type.
member.typeparam: The pipeline behavior type.
The message type.
The result type.
member.returns: The current instance of for chaining.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IMediatorServiceBuilder.RegisterNotificationBehavior``2
member.summary: Registers a notification-specific pipeline behavior. Provides a symmetric registration experience to for notification pipelines.
member.typeparam: The implementation type.
The notification message type.
member.returns: The current instance of for chaining.

member.name: T:NetMediate.IMessage
member.summary: Represents a message that can be handled or processed by a mediator or messaging infrastructure.
member.remarks: Implement this interface to define a message type for use with mediator patterns or message-based systems. This interface serves as a marker and does not specify any members.

member.name: T:NetMediate.INotification
member.summary: Defines a notification message that can be published to multiple handlers.
member.remarks: Implement this interface to represent messages that are intended to be broadcast to one or more recipients without expecting a response. Notifications are typically used for event-driven communication within an application.

member.name: T:NetMediate.INotificationHandler`1
member.summary: Defines a handler for notification messages that do not return a result.
member.remarks: Notification handlers are typically used to process events or signals that may be handled by zero or more handlers. Unlike request handlers, notification handlers do not return a value to the sender.
member.typeparam: The type of notification message to handle. Must not be null.

member.name: M:NetMediate.Internals.Extensions.RegisterPipelineCacheClearing(System.Action{System.IServiceProvider})
member.summary: Called once per closed-generic executor type (from a static constructor) to register the cache-clearing action for that type's pre-compiled pipeline cache.

member.name: M:NetMediate.Internals.Extensions.ClearCache(System.IServiceProvider)
member.summary: Clears the static handler cache. Optionally clears the behavior cache and pre-compiled pipeline caches for a specific provider instance. Intended for test isolation only — in production, prefer using distinct message types per handler registration to avoid cache contamination.

member.name: T:NetMediate.Internals.INotifiable
member.summary: Defines a contract for asynchronously dispatching or publishing notification messages to all registered handlers.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface provide mechanisms for notifying multiple handlers about events or messages. The order in which handlers are invoked is not guaranteed unless explicitly documented by the implementation. All notification operations are asynchronous and support cancellation via a cancellation token.

member.name: M:NetMediate.Internals.INotifiable.DispatchNotifications``1(System.Object,``0,NetMediate.INotificationHandler{``0}[],System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Dispatches a notification message directly to the provided handlers, bypassing the pipeline.
member.typeparam: The notification message type.
member.param: An optional key to distinguish this notification from others of the same message type.
The notification message instance.
The resolved handlers to invoke.
A token to observe for cancellation.
member.returns: A that completes when dispatch finishes.

member.name: M:NetMediate.Internals.INotifiable.Notify``1(System.Object,``0,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Publishes a single notification through the pipeline and all registered handlers.
member.typeparam: The notification message type.
member.param: An optional key to distinguish this notification from others of the same message type.
The notification message instance.
A token to observe for cancellation.
member.returns: A that completes when all handlers have been notified.

member.name: M:NetMediate.Internals.INotifiable.Notify``1(System.Object,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Publishes each message in the sequence through the pipeline and all registered handlers.
member.typeparam: The notification message type.
member.param: An optional key to distinguish this notification from others of the same message type.
The sequence of notification messages to publish.
A token to observe for cancellation.
member.returns: A that completes when all messages have been dispatched.

member.name: T:NetMediate.Internals.NotificationPipelineExecutor`1
member.summary: Specialized pipeline executor for notification handlers. Resolves (two-parameter), (one-parameter), and (notification shorthand) from DI, without needing a runtime type switch. Registered as a closed type by RegisterNotificationHandler<THandler, TMessage>(...) so that no open-generic typeof() resolver is needed.

member.name: M:NetMediate.Internals.NotificationPipelineExecutor`1.#ctor(System.IServiceProvider)
member.summary: Specialized pipeline executor for notification handlers. Resolves (two-parameter), (one-parameter), and (notification shorthand) from DI, without needing a runtime type switch. Registered as a closed type by RegisterNotificationHandler<THandler, TMessage>(...) so that no open-generic typeof() resolver is needed.

member.name: T:NetMediate.Internals.RequestPipelineExecutor`2
member.summary: Specialized pipeline executor for request handlers that also resolves registrations in addition to . This avoids the need for MakeGenericType and is fully AOT-compatible.

member.name: M:NetMediate.Internals.RequestPipelineExecutor`2.#ctor(System.IServiceProvider)
member.summary: Specialized pipeline executor for request handlers that also resolves registrations in addition to . This avoids the need for MakeGenericType and is fully AOT-compatible.

member.name: T:NetMediate.Internals.StreamPipelineExecutor`2
member.summary: Specialized pipeline executor for stream handlers that also resolves registrations in addition to . This avoids the need for MakeGenericType and is fully AOT-compatible.

member.name: M:NetMediate.Internals.StreamPipelineExecutor`2.#ctor(System.IServiceProvider)
member.summary: Specialized pipeline executor for stream handlers that also resolves registrations in addition to . This avoids the need for MakeGenericType and is fully AOT-compatible.

member.name: T:NetMediate.IPipelineBehavior`1
member.summary: Defines a behavior that can be added to the processing pipeline for a message, allowing custom logic to be executed before or after the main message handler.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface can be used to add cross-cutting concerns, such as logging, validation, or exception handling, to the message processing pipeline. The behavior can invoke the next delegate to continue processing or short-circuit the pipeline as needed.
member.typeparam: The type of the message being processed. Must implement the IMessage interface and cannot be null.

member.name: T:NetMediate.IPipelineNotificationBehavior`1
member.summary: A dedicated shorthand for notification-specific pipeline behaviors, mirroring the symmetric registration experience of for requests.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface are registered via and are resolved exclusively for notification pipelines by NotificationPipelineExecutor<TMessage>. This provides a type-safe, AOT-compatible way to add cross-cutting concerns to notification dispatch without registering against the more general IPipelineBehavior<TMessage, Task>.
member.typeparam: The notification message type. Cannot be null.

member.name: T:NetMediate.IPipelineRequestBehavior`2
member.summary: Defines a behavior that can be added to the processing pipeline for a message, allowing custom logic to be executed before or after the main message handler.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface can be used to add cross-cutting concerns, such as logging, validation, or exception handling, to the message processing pipeline. The behavior can invoke the next delegate to continue processing or short-circuit the pipeline as needed.
member.typeparam: The type of the message being processed. Must implement the IMessage interface and cannot be null.
The type of the response returned by the pipeline after processing the message.

member.name: T:NetMediate.IPipelineStreamBehavior`2
member.summary: Defines a behavior that can be added to the processing pipeline for a message, allowing custom logic to be executed before or after the main message handler.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface can be used to add cross-cutting concerns, such as logging, validation, or exception handling, to the message processing pipeline. The behavior can invoke the next delegate to continue processing or short-circuit the pipeline as needed.
member.typeparam: The type of the message being processed. Must implement the IMessage interface and cannot be null.
The type of the response returned by the pipeline after processing the message.

member.name: T:NetMediate.IPipelineBehavior`2
member.summary: Defines a behavior that can be added to the processing pipeline for a message, allowing custom logic to be executed before or after the main message handler.
member.remarks: Implementations of this interface can be used to add cross-cutting concerns, such as logging, validation, or exception handling, to the message processing pipeline. The behavior can invoke the next delegate to continue processing or short-circuit the pipeline as needed.
member.typeparam: The type of the message being processed. Must implement the IMessage interface and cannot be null.
The type of the result returned by the pipeline after processing the message.

member.name: M:NetMediate.IPipelineBehavior`2.Handle(System.Object,`0,NetMediate.PipelineBehaviorDelegate{`0,`1},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Handles the specified message by invoking the next delegate in the processing pipeline.
member.param: An optional key to distinguish this message from others of the same type. This can be used for scenarios like notifications where multiple handlers may be involved.
The message to be processed by the handler.
A delegate representing the next handler to invoke in the pipeline.
A cancellation token that can be used to cancel the operation.
member.returns: The result produced by processing the message through the pipeline.

member.name: T:NetMediate.IRequest`1
member.summary: Defines a request message that expects a response of the specified type when processed.
member.remarks: Implement this interface to represent a request that is sent through a mediator or messaging pipeline and expects a response. The response type can be any type appropriate for the operation, including void (using Unit) if no response is needed.
member.typeparam: The type of the response that will be returned when the request is handled.

member.name: T:NetMediate.IRequestHandler`2
member.summary: Defines a handler for processing a request message and returning a response asynchronously.
member.remarks: Implement this interface to handle specific request messages and provide a response. Handlers are typically used in request/response messaging patterns to encapsulate business logic for processing requests.
member.typeparam: The type of the request message to handle. Must not be null.
The type of the response returned by the handler.

member.name: T:NetMediate.IStream`1
member.summary: Represents a message that supports streaming of response data of a specified type.
member.typeparam: The type of the response elements that are streamed by this message.

member.name: T:NetMediate.IStreamHandler`2
member.summary: Defines a handler for processing streaming messages and producing a sequence of responses asynchronously.
member.remarks: Implement this interface to handle messages that require asynchronous streaming of multiple responses. The handler processes the incoming message and returns an representing the response stream.
member.typeparam: The type of the streaming message to handle. Must not be null.
The type of the responses produced by the handler.

member.name: T:NetMediate.ITerminator
member.summary: Defines a contract for terminating an operation or process.
member.remarks: Implementations should provide logic to safely and reliably terminate the associated operation. The specific behavior of termination depends on the implementing type and may include resource cleanup or cancellation of ongoing work.

member.name: M:NetMediate.ITerminator.Terminate
member.summary: Performs a termination operation, ending the current process or activity.
member.remarks: Call this method to explicitly terminate the associated process or workflow. The specific effects of termination depend on the implementation. After calling this method, further operations may not be possible.

member.name: T:NetMediate.KeyedServiceAttribute
member.summary: Specifies a key that can be used to identify or distinguish a service implementation for dependency injection or service location.
member.remarks: Apply this attribute to a class to associate it with a specific key, enabling keyed resolution scenarios in dependency injection frameworks or custom service locators. The key can be any object and is typically used to differentiate between multiple implementations of the same service contract.

member.name: P:NetMediate.KeyedServiceAttribute.Key
member.summary: Gets or sets the key associated with the current object.

member.name: T:NetMediate.MediatorException
member.summary: Represents an exception thrown when a mediator handler or pipeline behavior fails, carrying structured context about the originating message type, handler type, and distributed-tracing activity ID.

remarks.para: Instances of this exception are created automatically by the mediator when a handler or behavior throws an unhandled exception during or Request calls. The original exception is preserved as .
Notification dispatch is fire-and-forget; handler exceptions there are logged rather than surfaced as .

member.name: P:NetMediate.MediatorException.MessageType
member.summary: Gets the CLR type of the message that was being dispatched when the exception occurred.

member.name: P:NetMediate.MediatorException.HandlerType
member.summary: Gets the CLR type of the handler interface for which dispatch was attempted (e.g. ICommandHandler<TMessage>, IRequestHandler<TMessage,TResponse>). May be when the handler type cannot be determined.

member.name: P:NetMediate.MediatorException.TraceId
member.summary: Gets the of the ambient at the time the exception was captured, or when no activity was active.

member.name: M:NetMediate.MediatorException.#ctor(System.Type,System.Type,System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of .
member.param: The message type being dispatched.
The handler interface type, or .
The activity trace ID, or .
The original exception thrown by the handler or behavior.

member.name: T:NetMediate.MediatorExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for the IMediator interface to facilitate sending request and streaming messages.
member.remarks: These extension methods offer a simplified way to send requests and initiate streaming operations using an IMediator instance. They support both standard request/response and streaming scenarios, allowing for asynchronous and cancellable operations.

member.name: M:NetMediate.MediatorExtensions.<G>$DA0B55F6AFB7B7467588BB90ED3EF929.Request``1(NetMediate.IRequest{``0},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends a request to a handler and awaits a response.
member.remarks: This method uses reflection () internally to dispatch the request. It is not compatible with NativeAOT or trimming. Prefer the IMediator.Request{TMessage, TResponse} overload which specifies both type arguments explicitly.
member.typeparam: The response type.
member.param: Request instance.
Cancellation token.
member.returns: The handler response.

member.name: M:NetMediate.MediatorExtensions.<G>$DA0B55F6AFB7B7467588BB90ED3EF929.Request``1(System.Object,NetMediate.IRequest{``0},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends a request to a handler and awaits a response.
member.remarks: This method uses reflection () internally to dispatch the request. It is not compatible with NativeAOT or trimming. Prefer the IMediator.Request{TMessage, TResponse} overload which specifies both type arguments explicitly.
member.typeparam: The response type.
member.param: An optional key to distinguish this request from others of the same message type.
Request instance.
Cancellation token.
member.returns: The handler response.

member.name: M:NetMediate.MediatorExtensions.<G>$DA0B55F6AFB7B7467588BB90ED3EF929.RequestStream``1(NetMediate.IStream{``0},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends a request to a handler and receives a stream of responses asynchronously.
member.remarks: This method uses reflection () internally to dispatch the stream request. It is not compatible with NativeAOT or trimming. Prefer the IMediator.RequestStream{TMessage, TResponse} overload which specifies both type arguments explicitly.
member.typeparam: The response item type.
member.param: Stream request instance.
Cancellation token.
member.returns: An asynchronous response stream.

member.name: M:NetMediate.MediatorExtensions.<G>$DA0B55F6AFB7B7467588BB90ED3EF929.RequestStream``1(System.Object,NetMediate.IStream{``0},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends a request to a handler and receives a stream of responses asynchronously.
member.remarks: This method uses reflection () internally to dispatch the stream request. It is not compatible with NativeAOT or trimming. Prefer the IMediator.RequestStream{TMessage, TResponse} overload which specifies both type arguments explicitly.
member.typeparam: The response item type.
member.param: An optional key to distinguish this request from others of the same message type.
Stream request instance.
Cancellation token.
member.returns: An asynchronous response stream.

member.name: T:NetMediate.MessageValidationException
member.summary: Represents an exception that is thrown when a message fails validation.
member.remarks: Use this exception to indicate that a message did not meet validation requirements. The associated ValidationResult provides details about the validation failure.
member.param: The result of the validation operation that caused the exception. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:NetMediate.MessageValidationException.#ctor(System.ComponentModel.DataAnnotations.ValidationResult)
member.summary: Represents an exception that is thrown when a message fails validation.
member.remarks: Use this exception to indicate that a message did not meet validation requirements. The associated ValidationResult provides details about the validation failure.
member.param: The result of the validation operation that caused the exception. Cannot be null.

member.name: P:NetMediate.MessageValidationException.ValidationResult
member.summary: Gets the result of the validation operation.

member.name: T:NetMediate.NetMediateDI
member.summary: Provides the internal extension method used by source-generated code to register NetMediate services with the dependency injection container.

remarks.para: Do not call directly. Use the AddNetMediate() extension method emitted by the NetMediate.SourceGeneration source generator instead. That method is generated at compile time inside your project and calls this method with all discovered handlers registered in an AOT-safe, trim-safe manner.

member.name: M:NetMediate.NetMediateDI.UseNetMediate(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.Action{NetMediate.IMediatorServiceBuilder})
member.summary: Configures NetMediate core services and applies the provided explicit handler registration callback. This overload is intended to be called by source-generated code only — prefer the generated AddNetMediate() extension method over calling this method directly.
member.remarks: This overload is AOT- and trim-safe when all handler types are explicitly registered inside . It does not scan assemblies for handlers. Subsequent calls on the same are silently ignored.
member.param: The service collection to add NetMediate services to.
The callback that performs explicit handler and behavior registrations.
member.returns: A instance for additional configuration.

member.name: M:NetMediate.NetMediateDI.UseNetMediate``1(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.Action{NetMediate.IMediatorServiceBuilder})
member.summary: Configures NetMediate core services with a custom notifier type and applies the provided explicit handler registration callback. This overload is intended to be called by source-generated code only.
member.typeparam: The type implementing to use for notification handling.
member.param: The service collection to add NetMediate services to.
The callback that performs explicit handler and behavior registrations.
member.returns: A instance for additional configuration.

member.name: T:NetMediate.PipelineBehaviorDelegate`2
member.summary: Represents a delegate that handles a message asynchronously.
member.remarks: Use this delegate to define custom asynchronous logic for processing messages as fire (and may forget [notifications]). The operation may be canceled by the provided cancellationToken.
member.typeparam: The type of the message to handle.
The type of the response data.
member.param: An optional key to distinguish this handler from others of the same interface type.
The notification message to process.
A cancellation token that can be used to cancel the operation.
member.returns: A TResult that represents the asynchronous handling operation.
